expectations.text: Correctly identifies the system as high-risk via Annex I Path A (Art. 6(1)) because Regulation 2017/745 (Medical Devices) is listed in Annex I Section A
expectations.evidence: Response incorrectly references 'Annex I, Section 5' and 'Annex II' (not Annex I) as where MDR is listed. The actual EU AI Act lists MDR in Annex I Section A. The response states 'Annex II of the EU AI Act specifically lists EU MDR (Regulation 2017/745)' which is factually incorrect — it is Annex I. The Path A classification concept is correct but the specific Annex reference is wrong.

expectations.text: Explains that MDR CE marking does NOT automatically satisfy AI Act compliance requirements
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly states in the Executive Summary: 'The CE marking obtained under the EU Medical Device Regulation (MDR, Regulation 2017/745) does not automatically satisfy EU AI Act requirements' and Section 2.3 lists specific gaps the MDR assessment does not cover.

expectations.text: Explains that AI Act requirements (Arts. 9-17) must be integrated into the existing MDR conformity assessment procedure
expectations.evidence: Response states in section 4.3: 'the same Notified Body is expected to verify AI Act compliance as part of the integrated assessment'. Section 3.1 covers provider obligations including Arts. 9-17 requirements. The integration approach is correctly described.

expectations.text: States the 2 August 2028 deadline specifically for Annex I safety component systems (extended by the Digital Omnibus adopted June 29, 2026)
expectations.evidence: Response states the transitional period ends in 'August 2027' (section 5.2: 'allowing continued use until August 2027'). The 2 August 2028 deadline confirmed by the Digital Omnibus is not mentioned. The Digital Omnibus is not referenced at all.

expectations.text: Identifies hospital deployer obligations under Art. 26 (instructions compliance, staff competence, monitoring, log retention)
expectations.evidence: Section 3.2 covers deployer obligations including: instructions compliance (Art. 26(1)), human oversight (Art. 26(2)), monitoring (Art. 26(5)), record-keeping with '6 months' log retention (Art. 26(6)). Staff competence (Art. 26(4)) also mentioned via training requirements.
